title: The Temporal Sequence of Party Leader Incongruence
description: This dataset comprises high-density EEG recordings from 44 participants in a preregistered study investigating partisan processing of political incongruence. Using a multitask design with 'Just Faces' and 'Statements & Faces' tasks, the study disentangles neural responses to party leader identity and message content incongruence, testing the 'Hot Cognition' hypothesis. The dataset includes raw 64-channel EEG data, behavioral ratings, event annotations, political leader stimuli, and preprocessed derivatives.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 64-channel BioSemi ActiveTwo system with International 10-20 electrode layout, including EOG, ECG, and mastoid leads. Recordings were referenced to CMS/DRL and re-referenced to average reference during preprocessing. Data were band-pass filtered offline (0.5–40 Hz). Two experimental tasks were employed: (1) Just Faces task with 260 trials of 700ms face presentations to measure identity incongruence responses, and (2) Statements & Faces task with 240 trials using a 2×2 factorial design (In-party/Out-party × Pro-attitudinal/Counter-attitudinal) with statement durations of 1830–2880ms followed by face presentations of 1200–1400ms. Stimuli were presented using PsychoPy and analyzed with MNE-Python.

funding_references.award_title: IP-PAD: Horizon Europe MSCA Doctoral Networks
